global_contract.generic_claim_policy: A slide fails if it only says the workflow is better without naming product input, mechanism, generated output, comparison, verification, or repair behavior.
global_contract.output_claim_policy: The product must be described as code-generated editable PPT. Image generation is allowed only for backgrounds or illustrations.

slide_message_contracts.reader_question: What is Vulca and what concrete product output does this deck demonstrate?
slide_message_contracts.required_answer: Vulca converts a real commercial brief plus selected multimodal tutorial and case data into a code-generated editable PPT deck with trace, QA, and review artifacts.
slide_message_contracts.visible_product_object: input-to-output product surface showing commercial brief, selected source pack, generated deck preview, trace manifest, and release gate
must_say: the main output is editable PPT, not only an image
the deck is generated from a selected source pack and workflow
public release is blocked until QA and human review pass

slide_message_contracts.reader_question: How does Vulca transform raw business and tutorial material into design instructions?
slide_message_contracts.required_answer: The system selects a usecase and allowed source pack, derives product capability memory, then turns it into per-slide message contracts and workflow gates before generation.
slide_message_contracts.visible_product_object: source-to-memory pipeline with selected usecase id, allowed source records, capability graph, and slide contract cards
must_say: tutorials and videos are not copied into slides
derived observations become product memory and gates
weak content is repaired by adding stricter contracts

slide_message_contracts.reader_question: Why is this different from prompt-only, image-only, template, design-editor, or office-copilot slide creation?
slide_message_contracts.required_answer: Vulca is differentiated by the case database, skill workflow, bad-control comparison, trace manifest, and code-generated editable PPT output, not by simply producing a nicer-looking slide.
slide_message_contracts.visible_product_object: competitor boundary matrix with five alternatives and the specific Vulca mechanism each lacks
must_say: image generation is not the main product output
template and editor tools do not prove a case-pack-to-code path

slide_message_contracts.reader_question: How do we know the product really used the data and workflow instead of producing another direct deck?
slide_message_contracts.required_answer: Each full-arm slide must carry capability ids, message contract id, workflow gate id, competitor boundary ids, content specificity status, and required product terms in trace before native PPT drawing.
slide_message_contracts.visible_product_object: trace and QA inspection board with full-arm pass fields and bad-control failure fields
must_say: trace fields prove the full arm consumed the content layer
the bad control can see Run 2.56 but cannot use Run 2.57
generic claims fail before renderer execution

slide_message_contracts.reader_question: What is the strongest product moment the public demo should remember?
slide_message_contracts.required_answer: The strongest moment is a real brief becoming an editable PPT system: data, memory, workflow gates, code modules, preview, trace, and QA all shown as one product operating loop.
slide_message_contracts.visible_product_object: large product operating-loop stage with brief, database, design memory, code renderer, editable PPT preview, trace, and review gate
must_say: the system is an operating loop, not a one-shot deck writer
code generation owns the editable slide structure
QA and release gates remain visible at the climax

slide_message_contracts.reader_question: What should happen next before we claim this product is publishable?
slide_message_contracts.required_answer: Run 2.57 is a content-layer repair only; Run 2.58 must generate a new four-arm PPT that consumes the content contracts, then pass visual review, native render inspection, source-boundary review, and human approval.
slide_message_contracts.visible_product_object: release decision wall with current latest generated run, new content layer, next generated run, blocked gates, and human review status
must_say: 2.58 must consume the content layer before native drawing
public release remains blocked until review gates pass
